findings: Config and frozen cohort manifests are synchronized at 35 cohorts / 5,922 samples.
All paper-facing target signatures are biologically grounded, non-synthetic panels.
The broader 30-signature benchmark still contains 19 explicit synthetic control signatures; they are benchmark controls, not headline evidence.
No unexpected synthetic/stub/mock keywords were found in the runtime code, paper, or skill surface.

all_hits.text: KEYWORD_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\b(synthetic|stub|fake|mock|toy|placeholder|dummy)\b", re.IGNORECASE)

findings.append("No unexpected synthetic/stub/mock keywords were found in the runtime code, paper, or skill surface.")
f"- Synthetic control signatures in broader benchmark: `{signatures['synthetic_control_signature_count']}`",

The broader 30-signature benchmark also contains 19 explicit synthetic control signatures used as benchmark scaffolding for brittle, mixed, confounded, and insufficient-coverage behavior. Those synthe
- The broader 30-signature benchmark still contains explicit synthetic control signatures, but the paper-facing target panel, external validations, and prospective case studies use only non-synthetic
- the paper-facing target signatures listed in `config/paper_target_signatures.tsv` are all non-synthetic
- the broader benchmark’s synthetic controls are confined to documented control panels
- no unexpected `synthetic` / `stub` / `mock` placeholders leak into the runtime code, paper, or skill surface
- Of those 30 signatures, 11 are biologically grounded benchmark targets and 19 are explicit synthetic benchmark controls for brittle, mixed, confounded, and low-coverage behavior

The benchmark answers: is a transcriptomic signature reproducible **within** its native biological program, and does it correctly fail **outside** it? We use Cochran's Q decomposition (Q_within / Q_be

Verifies that the active scored cohort panel is the real 35-cohort GEO freeze, that the paper-facing target signatures are all non-synthetic, that no unexpected `synthetic` / `stub` / `mock` placehold
2. Run the provenance audit and confirm `outputs/canonical_v8/provenance_audit.json` reports the 35-cohort / 5,922-sample real-data panel and non-synthetic paper target signatures

brittle_small_study_inflammation	primary	brittle	Random subset of inflammatory genes plus non-specific housekeeping noise; mimics underpowered single-study extraction with no validation cohort.	inflam

brittle_platform_specific	primary	brittle	Ribosomal protein and mitochondrial genes known to vary by platform (array vs RNA-seq) and library prep; expected to show strong batch effects.	technical_arti

brittle_single_tissue	primary	brittle	Liver-specific genes (albumin, apolipoproteins, CYPs) mixed with generic immune markers; expected to fail in non-hepatic cohorts.	tissue_specific	Synthetic brittl
brittle_overfit_noise	primary	brittle	Random assortment of genes with no shared biological pathway or coherent regulation; mimics overfit feature selection from high-dimensional noise.	none	Synthetic

mixed_emt_inflammation	primary	mixed	Combines EMT mesenchymal markers with inflammatory cytokines; replicates in fibrosis and wound healing but fails in sepsis and pure immune activation.	emt	Syntheti

mixed_hypoxia_stress	primary	mixed	Combines hypoxia-response genes with stress/UPR markers; replicates in solid tumors but inconsistent in hematologic or non-hypoxic inflammatory contexts.	hypoxia	Syn

mixed_senescence_proliferation	primary	mixed	Combines senescence arrest markers (CDKN2A/1A) with residual proliferation genes; contradictory biology produces inconsistent cross-cohort scores.	senescen

confounded_proliferation	primary	confounded	Pure cell-cycle/mitotic genes; dominates any proliferative contrast but reflects nuisance biology rather than specific pathway activation.	proliferation	Syn

confounded_ribosomal	primary	confounded	Ribosomal protein genes (RPL/RPS family); universally expressed housekeeping genes that track sample quality and cell size, not specific biology.	technical_arti

confounded_immune_infiltrate	primary	confounded	Pure immune cell-type markers (T-cell, B-cell, monocyte, NK); tracks immune cell fraction rather than pathway activation within cells.	immune_infiltrati

stealth_confounded_hypoxia_prolif	primary	confounded	Hypoxia genes (VEGFA, LDHA, CA9) plus proliferation markers (MKI67, TOP2A, CDK1); real hypoxia signal contaminated by proliferation confounder. Sim

stealth_confounded_inflam_immune	primary	confounded	Inflammatory cytokines (IL6, TNF, CCL2) plus immune infiltration markers (CD3D, CD8A, PTPRC); real inflammatory signal contaminated by immune infilt

lowcov_olfactory_1	primary	insufficient_coverage	Olfactory receptor genes not present on standard expression platforms; expected to fail coverage threshold.	out_of_universe	Synthetic sentinel: olfacto

lowcov_olfactory_2	primary	insufficient_coverage	Second olfactory/taste receptor sentinel set; independent gene selection to confirm coverage rejection is robust.	out_of_universe	Synthetic sentinel: o

The conceptual backbone is the MSigDB Hallmark gene-set collection (Liberzon et al., "The Molecular Signatures Database Hallmark Gene Set Collection," Cell Systems 2015), supplemented by curated senes

Anchor family: synthetic brittle control mimicking underpowered single-study extraction. Extraction rule: random subset of 15 inflammatory-response genes (IL1B, CXCL2, CCL4, PTGS2, ICAM1, SOD2, PLAUR,

Anchor family: synthetic brittle control for platform-dependent expression artifacts. Extraction rule: 10 ribosomal protein genes (RPL13A, RPL7, RPL3, RPL11, RPL5, RPS4X, RPS27A, RPS3, RPS8, RPS18) pl

Anchor family: synthetic brittle control for tissue-specificity failure. Extraction rule: 13 liver-specific genes (ALB, APOA1, APOB, APOC3, CYP3A4, CYP2E1, CYP1A2, HP, TF, FGA, FGB, FGG, SERPINA1) plu

Anchor family: synthetic brittle control for overfit/noise selection. Extraction rule: 15 genes selected for large genomic size or high somatic mutation frequency (ANKRD36, ZNF518A, KIAA1549, OBSCN, S
Anchor family: synthetic mixed signature combining EMT and inflammatory biology. Extraction rule: 10 EMT markers (VIM, FN1, SNAI2, ZEB1, COL1A1, COL3A1, ACTA2, SPARC, FAP, LOXL2) plus 10 inflammatory

Anchor family: synthetic mixed signature combining hypoxia and stress/UPR response. Extraction rule: 10 hypoxia genes (VEGFA, SLC2A1, LDHA, CA9, BNIP3, ADM, NDRG1, LOX, HIF1A, PDK1) plus 10 stress/UPR

Anchor family: synthetic mixed signature combining senescence arrest and residual proliferation markers. Extraction rule: 10 senescence markers (CDKN2A, CDKN1A, TP53, GLB1, SERPINE1, IL6, CXCL8, IGFBP

Anchor family: synthetic negative control — pure proliferation/mitotic biology. Extraction rule: 20 core cell-cycle/mitotic genes (MKI67, TOP2A, PCNA, CCNB1, CDK1, CCNA2, BUB1, AURKA, PLK1, CDC20, CCN
Anchor family: synthetic negative control — ribosomal protein housekeeping genes. Extraction rule: 20 ribosomal protein genes (10 RPL + 10 RPS family members), all direction "up" with uniform weight.

Anchor family: synthetic negative control — immune cell-type deconvolution markers. Extraction rule: 20 immune lineage markers spanning T cells (CD3D, CD3E, CD8A, CD8B, CD4, FOXP3), B cells (MS4A1, CD

Anchor family: synthetic stealth confounded — real hypoxia signal contaminated by proliferation confounder. Extraction rule: 15 core hypoxia genes (VEGFA, SLC2A1, LDHA, CA9, BNIP3, ADM, NDRG1, LOX, HI

Anchor family: synthetic stealth confounded — real inflammatory signal contaminated by immune infiltration confounder. Extraction rule: 15 core inflammatory genes (IL6, IL1B, TNF, CXCL8, CCL2, CCL3, P
Anchor family: synthetic blind brittle control — neuronal/receptor genes with no coherent expression program in standard bulk-tissue cohorts. Extraction rule: 15 genes (ABCA7, GPR37, SLC17A7, CHRNA7,

Anchor family: synthetic blind mixed signature combining EMT markers with stress/UPR genes. Extraction rule: 10 EMT genes (VIM, FN1, SNAI1, ZEB2, COL1A2, COL5A1, ACTA2, TAGLN, POSTN, TGFBI) plus 10 st
Anchor family: synthetic blind confounded control — proliferation plus inflammation. Extraction rule: 10 proliferation genes (MKI67, TOP2A, PCNA, CCNB1, CDK1, CCNA2, BUB1, AURKA, PLK1, CDC20) plus 10
Anchor family: synthetic blind stealth confounded — EMT signal plus immune infiltration markers. Extraction rule: 15 EMT genes (VIM, FN1, SNAI2, ZEB1, COL1A1, COL3A1, ACTA2, SPARC, FAP, LOXL2, POSTN,
